title: Referential Integrity Management on ODBC
text: Referential Integrity Management on ODBC Referential integrity is handled by Uniface. Commit and Rollback The commit and rollback statements are supported provided the chosen DBMS supports these statements. Two-phase commit is not supported. Locking All Uniface locking types are supported if the DBMS supports them. The default locking type is optimistic. You can specify the locking type for tables that share the same ODBC Connector (for example OD1) by using the default locking connector option. However, if the locking type is specified in Uniface Application Model, it overrides any locking type specified using the default locking option. read/lock You can specify paranoid locking by using the read /lock ProcScript command in the read trigger of an entity. U_VERSION The ODBC Connector supports the U_VERSION mechanism to improve locking performance. It is strongly recommended that you use U_VERSION because this significantly reduces the chance of lock conflicts. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
